raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The section draws a broad inferential leap from two internal ablation comparisons on one benchmark to the claim that both components are 'confirmed essential ingredients'; the logical chain from those narrow results to that conclusion requires scrutiny.","R1.3":"'Substantially advances the state of the art' and 'confirms that both the contrastive objective and the information-gain formulation are essential' are strong claims resting solely on two ablations on a single benchmark (StackExchange-Tex); evidence-claim alignment is the primary review risk and is load-bearing.","R1.6":"'Review this section' plausibly covers sentence-level and rhetorical quality; the closing paragraph in particular uses emphatic language ('substantially advances', 'confirms') that may warrant tightening regardless of the technical verdict."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize technical accuracy and claim validity (i.e., whether the experimental evidence supports the state-of-the-art and 'essential ingredients' assertions), or writing quality and clarity?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section draws a broad inferential leap from two internal ablation comparisons on one benchmark to the claim that both components are 'confirmed essential ingredients'; the logical chain from those narrow results to that conclusion requires scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: 'Substantially advances the state of the art' and 'confirms that both the contrastive objective and the information-gain formulation are essential' are strong claims resting solely on two ablations on a single benchmark (StackExchange-Tex); evidence-claim alignment is the primary review risk and is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' plausibly covers sentence-level and rhetorical quality; the closing paragraph in particular uses emphatic language ('substantially advances', 'confirms') that may warrant tightening regardless of the technical verdict.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical accuracy and claim validity (i.e., whether the experimental evidence supports the state-of-the-art and 'essential ingredients' assertions), or writing quality and clarity?
